quantisation: int8 (default; no per-node override in config.yaml)
licence: SPAN upstream is Apache-2.0. The .hbm is a derivative of the upstream weights; check their terms before commercial redistribution.

toolchain.note: Recorded so a future failure can be attributed. The host HBDK and the board HBRT must match; 3.7.0 host pairs with 4.0.5 board.

cosine.note: Standard three-layer gates. Not yet measured for this model in this repo -- see 'measured'.

task_metric.name: PSNR vs ground-truth HR (and vs a bicubic baseline the upscaler must beat)
task_metric.protocol: Take a real HR image, 4x-downscale (INTER_AREA) to make the LR input, tile+blend through the model, score PSNR against the true HR. Bicubic resize of the same LR is the floor. span_ref logic in export.py / a board harness does this.

measured.note: No PSNR figure was recorded in the salvaged span material; left null rather than fabricated. The reference harness prints model-PSNR, bicubic-PSNR and their delta -- capture those on a rebuild.

The salvaged span conversion material carried NO board measurements (no latency, no FPS, no cosine, no PSNR). Every such field is null and must be filled from a rebuild + board run; none is fabricated here.
measured.hbm_mb_note: Approximate, from the repo-wide observation that a super-res .hbm is mostly instruction stream and scales with input AREA: this net is ~37 MB at a 128x128 tile and ~148 MB at 256x256, for identical per-pixel throughput. Not independently re-measured in this recipe.

notes.output_convention: SPAN normalises its input by (x-mean)*img_range and never undoes it, and ships no inference script saying what the raw output means. export.py scores both candidate conventions ('raw already [0,1]' vs 'raw/255 + mean') against a real HR image and folds the winner into the graph so the model returns [0,1]. Guessing this wrong yields a plausible image with shifted colour/levels at full speed.
notes.conv3xc: Conv3XC reparameterises a 1x1->3x3->1x1 branch + 1x1 skip into a single 3x3, but recomputes that fusion inside forward(). export.py fuses once, swaps forward() for the plain eval conv, and asserts the swap is numerically identical (<1e-5) before exporting.
notes.tile_area: The .hbm scales with tile AREA, not weights. Compile the 128 tile if the runtime tiles the frame.
notes.keep_both_pair: SPAN is the FIDELITY choice: it wins on clean input and is ~1/6 the size of Real-ESRGAN Compact (the sibling superres build). Compact is the PERCEPTUAL choice for blurred/compressed input. They are a keep-both pair, not better/worse.

status.recipe: export.py + config.yaml + compile.sh are reconstructed from the salvaged span convert scripts. calib.py is RECONSTRUCTED by analogy (no calibration script survived for span); it mirrors the sibling super-res calib and span_ref preprocessing.
status.verified: Not re-run end to end in this repo. No board close-out; no A/B/C cosine, no PSNR, no latency recorded.
